_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD / DNB / DCH or equivalent (Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_indications: Coombs-negative hemolytic anemia (early manifestation – increasing pallor)

ppd_preauth.q: Is the Serum ceruloplasmin levels depressed (<20 mg/dL)?
Is the 24-hour urine copper levels elevated (>100 µg/day)?

cpd_claim.q: Do Detailed ICPs contain a detailed line of treatment?

common_queries: Serum ceruloplasmin levels not depressed (<20 mg/dL) or not documented
24-hour urine copper levels not elevated (>100 µg/day) or not documented
Slit lamp examination for Kayser-Fleischer ring not performed or documented when clinically indicated
Age of patient is not below 3 years without sufficient justification
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) do not include a detailed line of treatment

clinical_key_pointers: Hematological manifestation: Coombs-negative hemolytic anemia (early manifestation – increasing pallor)
Hepatic manifestations (usually in early childhood): Persistent or recurrent Jaundice, Spider hemangioma, Portal hypertension, Fulminant hepatic failure
Neuropsychiatric manifestations: Tremors, Worsening handwriting, Decline in school performance, Dystonia, Psychiatric disturbances, Behavioral changes, Parkinsonism
